mappings.rationale: The section explicitly discusses archaeological sources for understanding pre-Inca cultures, including Chavin, Paracas, Nazca, and Moche sites.
Pizarro's conquest of the Incas (1532-40), the execution of Atahuallpa, and Spanish consolidation of power are directly covered in this section.
The Inca Empire's administrative hierarchy, taxation system, and territorial conquests represent a major pre-Columbian imperial system.
The Late Preceramic and Initial Period cultures, including the development of agriculture and early Andean settlements, connect to broader prehistorical questions.
The ethnography of the Andean region, Incan government and society, and the quipu system of numerical records involve anthropological analysis.
Documentary historical sources about the Incas and the Spanish conquest are part of the colonial Latin American literary and historical record.
The section raises historiographic problems in understanding Andean civilization, connecting to broader methodological questions in the discipline of history.
